title: xyris correlliorum
intro: Xyris correlliorum, also known as the Yellow-eyed Grass or Jamaican Yellow-eyed Grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae. The plant is native to Jamaica and other parts of the Caribbean, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Xyris correlliorum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Xyris correlliorum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies, although its use and preparation may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
